structure.modification: H intercalated in rocksalt LaO layer at (0.5, 0.5, 0)
structure.space_group: P4/mmm (symmetry lowered from I4/mmm by H ordering)

h_plus_intercalation.effect: Electron doping -- pushes toward Mott insulator
h_plus_intercalation.sc_prospect: POOR: Ni(+2) is insulating, not superconducting

h_minus_intercalation.effect: Hole doping -- pushes away from Mott transition
h_minus_intercalation.sc_prospect: MARGINAL: metallic but weaker correlations at Ni(+3)

half_h_option.note: Partial intercalation could maintain closer-to-optimal doping

doping_analysis.key_finding: H intercalation changes Ni valence; must be carefully controlled to maintain SC state

h_environment.site: tetrahedral interstitial in LaO rocksalt layer
h_environment.bonding: H likely forms O-H bond (0.79 A is typical O-H distance)

concerns: H intercalation changes Ni valence (either +2.0 or +3.0 depending on H charge state)
Optimal Ni valence for SC is +2.5; any H content disrupts this
H diffusion in the rocksalt layer may be fast at T > 200 K
Structural distortion from H ordering may lower symmetry significantly
15 GPa pressure needed for parent SC may not be compatible with H intercalation

h_mode_phonon.note: Wide range reflects uncertainty in H bonding character (O-H vs La-H vs mixed)

stability.phonon_gate: CONDITIONAL PASS -- H local modes are positive-frequency; need DFT confirmation
stability.overall_verdict: CONDITIONAL PASS -- E_hull within gate, phonon stability plausible
caveats: E_hull = 27 meV/atom is BELOW 50 meV/atom gate but with significant uncertainty
H charge state (H+ vs H-) critically affects Ni valence and superconductivity
Metastability against H2 release must be confirmed with DFT NEB barrier calculation
15 GPa pressure environment for SC may alter H stability

h_plus.d_z2_sigma_bond: PRESERVED (H is far from bilayer bridge)
h_plus.spin_fluctuations: ENHANCED (closer to Mott, but may open gap)
h_plus.sc_prospect: POOR: too close to Mott insulator, SC likely destroyed

h_minus.orbital_character: Ni d_z2 + d_{x2-y2} (broader bands, less correlated)
h_minus.d_z2_sigma_bond: PRESERVED (H is far from bilayer bridge)
h_minus.sc_prospect: MARGINAL: metallic but reduced correlations; lambda_sf likely lower

partial_h_0.5.sc_prospect: BEST CASE: may retain enough correlations for SC while adding H phonons

electronic.d_z2_sigma_bond: PRESERVED in all scenarios (H is in rocksalt, 4-5 A from bilayer bridge)
electronic.bilayer_coupling: MAINTAINED (inner apical O-Ni bond unaffected by H in rocksalt layer)
electronic.d_wave_pairing_prospect: CONDITIONAL: depends on maintaining Ni d_{x2-y2} AF correlations
electronic.key_finding: H intercalation in rocksalt layer preserves bilayer sigma-bonding; doping effect is the main risk; partial intercalation (H0.5) is optimal strategy

omega_log.method: Two-component model alpha2F: oxide (50 meV) + H mode (150 meV)

phase59_candidates_omega_log.name: Hg1223-H
phase59_candidates_omega_log.note: HYPOTHETICAL -- candidate failed stability gate in Phase 59

phase59_candidates_omega_log.name: [CuO2]2/[LiH]1
phase59_candidates_omega_log.note: HYPOTHETICAL -- candidate failed stability gate in Phase 59

phase59_candidates_omega_log.name: [CuO2]2/[LiH]2
phase59_candidates_omega_log.note: HYPOTHETICAL -- candidate failed stability gate in Phase 59

arithmetic.formula: omega_log = exp[ f_oxide * ln(omega_oxide) + f_H * ln(omega_H) ]

omega_log.key_finding: omega_log = 852 K at f_H = 0.35 PASSES 800 K gate. Minimum f_H for 800 K gate: 0.29. H modes at 150 meV (1741 K) boost omega_log from 250 K to 852 K (+241%).
